primary_operations.name: get
primary_operations.sdk_call: Request req = new Request.Builder().url(this.baseUrl + url).build(); try (Response r = this.client.newCall(req).execute()) { return r.body() == null ? "" : r.body().string(); }

primary_operations.name: post
primary_operations.sdk_call: RequestBody rb = RequestBody.create(body, MediaType.get("application/json")); Request req = new Request.Builder().url(this.baseUrl + url).post(rb).build(); try (Response r = this.client.newCall(req).execute()) { return r.body() == null ? "" : r.body().string(); }

primary_operations.name: delete
primary_operations.sdk_call: Request req = new Request.Builder().url(this.baseUrl + url).delete().build(); try (Response r = this.client.newCall(req).execute()) { /* ignore body */ }

code_style_notes: OkHttpClient is thread-safe; share one instance across the application.
Always close the Response in a try-with-resources block.
Use Interceptor to add auth headers.
